seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: input: replace the forest-graph radius cover with a large weighted GEOMETRIC covering instance on a dig grid (per-site installation costs + Chebyshev-radius coverage, artifacts are a subset of the candidate sites), turning min-cardinality tower placement into weighted geometric set cover / uncapacitated facility location.
title: Watchbeacon Coverage of an Archaeology Dig Grid
strategies: trivial baseline: install a beacon on every artifact cell (always feasible, ratio 0.1)
greedy weighted set cover: repeatedly install the candidate maximizing (newly-recorded artifacts / cost) until all artifacts are recorded
greedy + redundancy pruning: run greedy, then drop, cost-descending, any beacon whose recorded artifacts are all still covered by others
local search / LP-rounding: cluster-aware placement and swap moves to trade several small beacons for one wider cheaper one
